meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Vocabulary Set 2
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Part 1 words focus: thumbs up, art, music, learn, math, problems, important, think.

questions.question: What does 'thumbs up' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Thumbs up' means approval or agreement — 'I like this!' or 'I support this!' The title 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' means the author strongly approves of art and music in school.

questions.question: 'Art' refers to creative activities like drawing, painting, and sculpting.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Art' means creative work that expresses ideas and emotions — like drawing, painting, sculpture, and crafts. Art class is where students create original works of art.

questions.question: In art class, students can _______ how to draw, paint, and create.
questions.hint: To gain new knowledge or skills
questions.explanation: 'Learn' means to gain new knowledge or skills. In art class, students learn — they develop new abilities and understanding.

questions.question: What does 'important' mean?
options: Something that matters a lot and should not be missed
questions.explanation: 'Important' means something that matters greatly and should be taken seriously. The text says art is 'important' — it is valuable and should be part of school.

questions.question: We have four math _______ for homework. We must solve each question.
questions.explanation: 'Problems' in this context means math questions or exercises — not something bad. A 'math problem' is a question you need to solve.

questions.question: Art makes you '_______' — it uses your brain to come up with ideas. What does 'think' mean here?
options: To use your brain to create ideas and solve problems
questions.explanation: 'Think' means to use your brain to form ideas and reason. When you create art, you 'think' — you make creative decisions about color, shape, and design.

questions.question: 'Music' is only about singing — it has nothing to do with instruments.
questions.explanation: 'Music' includes singing, playing instruments, reading notes, rhythm, melody, and much more. Music class teaches all aspects of making and understanding music.

questions.question: The teacher says it is _______ to always do your best — it matters a lot.
questions.hint: Matters a lot; should not be ignored
questions.explanation: 'Important' means something that matters greatly. The teacher is saying that doing your best is significant — it is not something to ignore.

questions.question: Sort these Part 1 words by part of speech.
questions.explanation: 'Art,' 'music,' and 'problems' are things (nouns). 'Learn' and 'think' are actions (verbs). 'Important' describes how significant something is (adjective).

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'learn' correctly?
options: She learns to play guitar — she practices every day.
They learn art a thumbs up.
questions.explanation: 'Learns to play guitar' — she is gaining the skill of guitar playing through practice. 'Learn' is followed by what the person is gaining the ability to do.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence from the text:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Art is important.' Simple but powerful — the text argues directly that art matters. 'Important' is the predicate adjective describing 'Art.'

questions.question: 'Problems' always means something bad or wrong.
questions.explanation: 'Problems' has two meanings! It can mean 'something bad' (like a problem that needs fixing), OR 'math questions / exercises' (like math problems on a test). Context tells you which meaning is correct.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses BOTH 'art' and 'think' correctly:
options: She thinks art is one of the most important subjects in school.
Think the art is a thumbs up problem.
The math think was an art.
questions.explanation: 'She thinks' (uses her brain to believe) 'art is important' (a subject that matters). 'Think' here expresses a belief or opinion — a natural way to use both words together.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Music helps you get better at math.' The text claims that learning music improves mathematical thinking — one key argument for why music is important.

questions.question: A 'thumbs up' is a universal gesture meaning something is good or approved.
questions.explanation: Yes! In most cultures, a thumbs up means 'good,' 'I approve,' or 'yes!' The author uses 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' to express clear, enthusiastic support for these subjects.
